# tailor_auth_hook (Resource)

The auth_hook resource allows you to configure hooks that execute before specific auth events, such as login. This enables Just-In-Time (JIT) user provisioning and other pre-login processing.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `hook_point` (String) The hook point that determines when the hook executes. Currently only "before_login" is supported.
- `invoker` (Attributes) The invoker configuration for the hook. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--invoker))
- `namespace` (String) The auth namespace of the hook.
- `script_ref` (String) The name of the function registry entry to execute.
- `workspace_id` (String) The ID of the workspace that the auth hook belongs to.

### Optional

- `labels` (Map of String) Labels for the auth hook.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the resource.

<a id="nestedatt--invoker"></a>
### Nested Schema for `invoker`

Required:

- `machine_user` (Attributes) The machine user that invokes the hook function. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--invoker--machine_user))

<a id="nestedatt--invoker--machine_user"></a>
### Nested Schema for `invoker.machine_user`

Required:

- `auth_namespace` (String) The auth namespace of the machine user.
- `name` (String) The name of the machine user.
